argument: First action: verify 83(b) status and secure EIN exposure. EIN 37-2231884 is now a high-value attack surface. Immediate risks: phishing, synthetic identity fraud, IRS impersonation. Stripe Atlas automatically files 83(b) — manual submission already caused $150 loss (Class 19). Confirm no duplicate action was taken. EIN must not be logged, stored in plaintext, or exposed in version control. Rotate if leaked. Restrict access to `secrets/atlas` only. Second: file Delaware franchise tax — late fee starts at $200. Third: use EIN to activate Mercury business account, but only after confirming no ITIN is needed (C-corp = EIN suffices). Priority order: protect → comply → operate.
strongest_counter: Others may argue bank setup is fastest ROI, but unfrozen assets don't matter if the entity is compromised or fined.
